title: paronychia setigera subsp. setigera
intro: Paronychia setigera subsp. setigera is a perennial herb, belonging to the family Nyctaginaceae, commonly known as the four o'clock flower. The plant is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, and it has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The leaves and stems of Paronychia setigera subsp. setigera have been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin infections, and fever, due to its antibacterial and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to take advantage of its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
